article_items.title: How to Build a Custom Agent Harness
article_items.description: Effective agents are built with harnesses that are tightly coupled with the task at hand. The easiest way to build a custom harness is with LangChain's create_agent plus middleware. This guide covers the core agent loop and how you can customize it for your agent's use case.
article_items.excerpt: Effective agents are built with harnesses that are tightly coupled with the task at hand. The easiest way to build a custom harness is with LangChain's create_agent plus middleware. This guide covers the core agent loop and how you can customize it for your agent's use case.
article_items.summary: This includes runtime control over the agent itself: swapping the model based on task complexity, adjusting the prompt, and updating the agent’s message history (during compaction, for example). S e e w h a t y o u r a g e n t i s r e a l l y d o i n g LangSmith, our agent engineering platform, helps developers debug every agent decision, eval changes, and deploy in one click. A harness for a customer service agent looks very different from one built for a long-running coding agent. An agent is only as good as the context provided to the model The job of a harness is to provide context to the model at every step So, to build a useful agent, you need a harness that’s great at delivering the right.
This includes runtime control over the agent itself: swapping the model based on task complexity, adjusting the prompt, and updating the agent’s message history (during compaction, for example).

Pass in a model, tools, and a system prompt, and you have a working agent: Harnesses like Deep Agents and the Claude Agent SDK come pre-assembled with an opinionated middleware (explained below) stack: memory, context.
Middleware: how you customize the harness Middleware hooks into the agent loop at each step: before and after model calls, before and after tool calls, at agent startup and teardown.
At its core, an agent is a model calling tools in a loop until it completes a task and returns a result: The harness is the scaffolding around the model that connects it to the real world.
How to Build a Custom Agent Harness A harness is the scaffolding around the model that connects it to the real world.
Building useful agents is largely about customization: connecting your agent to the right context, data, and environment(s) for the task at hand.
Harness capabilities The job of a harness is to get the model the right context at the right time for the given task.
S e e w h a t y o u r a g e n t i s r e a l l y d o i n g LangSmith, our agent engineering platform, helps developers debug every agent decision, eval changes, and deploy in one click.
create_agent just implements the core agent loop, and it exposes middleware as a primitive for customization.
A harness for a customer service agent looks very different from one built for a long-running coding agent.

article_items.title: What do UK watchdog’s new rules on Google AI results mean for publishers?

article_items.summary: What has the CMA announced? It must allow publishers to block Google from using their content to power features such as AI Overviews and AI mode (an expanded version of Overviews). The global deployment underlines the impact of the CMA’s new digital competition powers.
